candidates.why: 'Total Revenue' is the consolidated top-line; the segment revenue lines are useful backups if naming changes.
Component of overall revenue; used as secondary priority after the main 'Total Revenue' line.
Explicit aggregate top-line revenue; best primary choice for REVT.
Finance income is non-operating, not revenue; excluded.
Interest income is non-operating, not operating revenue; excluded.
Net non-operating result, not revenue; excluded.
Bottom-line including OCI, not revenue; excluded.

candidates.row_label: Total comprehensive income for the period
candidates.why: Bottom-line including OCI, not revenue; excluded.
Incidental other income; not primary revenue line.
Alternative (non-GAAP) revenue measure; used only as backup after GAAP lines.
Operating profit, not revenue; excluded.

candidates.row_label: Impact of change in revenue recognition of Microsoft Enterprise Agreements
candidates.why: Adjustment item, not a standalone revenue line; excluded.

variables.notes: 'Total Revenue' is the explicit consolidated top-line and should be used first. The two detailed revenue lines are included as lower-priority backups in case reporting format changes. 'Non-GAAP Revenues' is added last as a fallback if GAAP lines are missing in some periods.

candidates.why: Primarily overhead/operating expenses for this software/services business; more appropriate for SG&A than direct COGS.
General operating overhead; better treated as SG&A.
Selling/admin overhead, not direct production costs.
Administrative overhead (SG&A), not direct cost of sales.
General operating leasing costs; overhead rather than direct COGS.
IT overhead; ambiguous but appears under operating expenses, not labeled as cost of sales.
Overhead-type operating expenses.
Selling expense (SG&A), not COGS.
Credit loss expense; not a direct cost of providing services.
Residual operating overhead; not explicitly cost of sales.
Facility overhead; SG&A-like rather than direct COGS.
Net operating total, not a COGS component.

candidates.row_label: Integration expenses, M&A and earn-out expenses
candidates.why: Non-recurring integration/M&A costs; not ongoing COGS.
Restructuring charges; not standard COGS.
Go-to-market restructuring; not standard COGS.
Balance sheet asset; not an income statement COGS line.
Current liability; not COGS line itself.
Liability; not the expense recognition line.

variables.notes: REVIEWED. The true direct costs of revenue are labeled as 'Cost of software purchased' and 'Third-party service delivery costs', which are outside the provided candidate list but clearly represent cost of sales for a software & cloud services business. Candidate lines are mainly SG&A-type operating expenses. Review is recommended to confirm that no additional direct cost components (e.g., revenue-related personnel) should be included in COGS. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Cost of software purchased, [Income Statement] Third-party service delivery costs

candidates.why: Major recurring overhead expense; classic SG&A component.
Bucket of miscellaneous overhead operating costs; SG&A component.
Explicit administrative overhead; SG&A component.
Income (negative expense); should not be treated as SG&A expense component.

variables.notes: REVIEWED. No single explicit 'SG&A' line exists, so SG&A is captured via its operating overhead components. Selected rows are recurring operating expenses that are not COGS, D&A, impairments, interest, or taxes, and they are not totals or balancing items. 'Other operating income' is excluded because it is income, not expense. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Travel and car expenses, [Income Statement] Expenses for short-term leases, [Income Statement] Information technology expenses, [Income Statement] Telecommunication expenses, [Income Statement] Marketing expenses, [Income Statement] Bad debt expenses, [Income Statement] Other expenses, [Income Statement] Maintenance and utility expenses

There is no explicit research and development line (e.g., 'Research and development', 'R&D') in the provided income statement preview, and no plausible proxy is labeled as such. XRD is therefore set to 0 (no separate R&D disclosure).

candidates.row_label: Gains from fair value remeasurement of previously held equity interest in a joint venture
candidates.why: Fair value gain on equity interests; not interest expense.
Primary explicit interest expense line; best first choice for XINT.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Interest component of lease liabilities; valid secondary form of interest expense.

candidates.row_label: Losses from fair value remeasurement of previously held equity interest
candidates.why: Fair value loss on equity interests; not interest expense.
Earnings attributable to NCI, unrelated to interest.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental disclosure of lease interest, duplicative of main lease interest line.
Balance sheet equity attributable to NCI, not interest.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities

variables.notes: REVIEWED. XINT is mapped to the main 'Interest expense' line, with 'Interest expense - Balancing value' and 'Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities' included as lower-priority alternatives to capture periods or formats where interest is split or renamed. Fair value gains/losses and non-controlling interest items are excluded as they are not interest costs. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Interest expense - Balancing value

candidates.row_label: Gains from fair value remeasurement of previously held equity interest in a joint venture
candidates.why: Income statement item; not an equity balance.

candidates.row_label: Losses from fair value remeasurement of previously held equity interest
candidates.why: Income statement item; not an equity balance.

candidates.row_label: Fair value loss from the valuation of equity instruments
candidates.why: Income statement item; not an equity balance.

candidates.row_label: Equity attributable to owners of the parent
candidates.why: Direct measure of book equity attributable to shareholders, excluding non-controlling interests; ideal for BE.
Includes non-controlling interests; only a fallback if parent equity not available.

final_choice.row_label: Equity attributable to owners of the parent

variables.notes: Book equity is taken as 'Equity attributable to owners of the parent', which excludes non-controlling interests and thus avoids double counting when MIB is added separately.

candidates.why: Income statement allocation of profit to NCI; not the balance sheet amount but confirms existence of NCI.
Balance sheet amount of non-controlling interests; appropriate for MIB.
Income statement NCI; again, flows not stock balance.

variables.notes: MIB is mapped to the balance sheet 'Non-controlling interest' line, which represents the stock of minority interest to be added to BE in the PROF denominator. Income statement NCI lines are not used for this purpose.
